goal: Can we implement those suggestions to put AI at the core

plan.goal: Reposition AI from opt-in sidebar to core workflow spine across 5 concrete UX shifts: (1) auto-run AI lint on save, (2) pre-fill reconciliation with AI suggestions by default, (3) expand team insights by default with manager AI summary header, (4) add freeform 'describe → structure' commit creation mode, (5) surface proactive risk signal banners on My Week. Steps 1-3 and 5 are pure frontend UX repositioning of existing backend capabilities. Step 4 requires a targeted backend extension: adding `suggestedChessPiece` to the existing `CommitDraftAssistResponse` DTO, prompt template, and service parser — no new endpoints, just one field added to an existing response.

steps.title: Auto-run AI lint on commit save instead of manual button click
steps.description:
Today AiLintPanel requires users to click 'Run AI Quality Check'. This step makes lint run automatically.

1. **Refactor AiLintPanel to accept an `autoRun` prop** (`frontend/src/components/ai/AiLintPanel.tsx`):
   - Add `autoRun?: boolean` prop (default `false` for backward compat).
   - When `autoRun` is `true`, trigger the lint API call on mount and whenever `planId` changes (using a `useEffect`).
   - Remove the 'Run AI Quality Check' button when `autoRun` is true; show results directly.
   - Keep the manual refresh button so users can re-run after edits.
   - Keep all existing error/unavailable/loading states.

2. **Update PreLockValidationPanel** (`frontend/src/components/lock/PreLockValidationPanel.tsx`):
   - Pass `autoRun={true}` to `<AiLintPanel>` so it fires immediately when the pre-lock panel opens.

3. **Add inline AiLintPanel to MyWeek page** (`frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx`):
   - Below the SoftWarningsPanel, render `<AiLintPanel planId={plan.id} userId={currentUserId} autoRun />` when the plan is in DRAFT state and `aiAssistanceEnabled` is true.
   - Wrap in the existing AiErrorBoundary pattern.
   - This means AI quality feedback appears as soon as commits exist — no button needed.

4. **Update tests** (`frontend/src/__tests__/PreLockValidation.test.tsx` and add test cases to `MyWeekPage.test.tsx`):
   - Verify AiLintPanel renders results without user clicking a button when `autoRun` is true.
   - Verify the manual button still works when `autoRun` is false.

steps.title: Pre-fill reconciliation page with AI-suggested outcomes by default
steps.description:
Today users see empty outcome dropdowns and must click 'AI Assist Reconciliation' to get suggestions. This step auto-loads AI suggestions when the reconcile page opens.

1. **Create a new hook `useAutoReconcileAssist`** in `frontend/src/api/aiHooks.ts`:
   - Accepts `planId` and `userId`.
   - On mount (when plan state is RECONCILING), automatically calls `aiApi.reconcileAssist({ planId, userId })`.
   - Returns `{ data: ReconcileAssistResponse | undefined, loading: boolean, error: string | null }`.
   - Gracefully handles AI unavailability (returns undefined data).

2. **Update ReconcilePage** (`frontend/src/routes/Reconcile.tsx`):
   - Call `useAutoReconcileAssist(planId, userId)` when the plan is in RECONCILING state.
   - When AI suggestions load, pre-populate `localOutcomes` and `localNotes` from `likelyOutcomes` — but mark them visually as 'AI-suggested' (not yet confirmed).
   - Show a prominent banner at the top: 'AI has pre-filled likely outcomes based on your ticket data and commit history. Review and confirm each outcome below.'
   - Each AI-prefilled outcome gets a subtle visual indicator (a small ✨ sparkle icon or 'AI suggested' badge next to the outcome selector).
   - The existing ReconcileAssistPanel button is replaced by the auto-loaded results integrated directly into the commit rows.
   - Carry-forward recommendations from AI appear as pre-checked checkboxes (user can uncheck).
   - Draft summary appears at the top of the page.
   - Keep AiFeedbackButtons on the overall suggestion.
   - If AI is unavailable, fall back silently to the current empty-state behavior.

3. **Create `AiSuggestedBadge` component** in `frontend/src/components/ai/AiSuggestedBadge.tsx`:
   - A small inline badge: sparkle icon + 'AI suggested' text.
   - Reusable across reconcile and future features.

4. **Update ReconcilePage tests** (`frontend/src/__tests__/ReconcilePage.test.tsx`):
   - Add tests verifying outcomes are pre-populated when AI data loads.
   - Add tests verifying fallback when AI is unavailable.
   - Add test verifying 'AI suggested' badge appears on prefilled outcomes.

steps.title: Expand team insights by default and add AI summary header to Team Week
steps.description:
Today team insights are collapsed behind a disclosure triangle (`insightsExpanded` starts `false`) and the manager AI summary endpoint exists but has no frontend consumer. This step makes AI the first thing managers see.

1. **Create `ManagerAiSummaryCard` component** in `frontend/src/components/ai/ManagerAiSummaryCard.tsx`:
   - Displays the AI-generated team summary as a prominent card at the top of Team Week.
   - Shows: `summaryText` as the main prose, `topRcdoBranches` as small badges, `unresolvedExceptionIds` count, `carryForwardPatterns` as bullet list, `criticalBlockedItemIds` count with warning styling.
   - Has a loading skeleton state, error state, and unavailable state.
   - Has AiFeedbackButtons on the suggestion.
   - Data shape matches `ManagerAiSummaryResponse` from the backend.

2. **Add `useManagerAiSummary` hook** in `frontend/src/api/aiHooks.ts`:
   - Calls `GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-summary` via the AI API.
   - Add the `getTeamAiSummary` method to `createAiApi` in `frontend/src/api/aiApi.ts` (the backend endpoint already exists).

3. **Update Team Week page** (`frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx`):
   - Change `insightsExpanded` default from `false` to `true`.
   - Add `<ManagerAiSummaryCard>` above the tab navigation, below the semantic search.
   - This makes the AI summary the first data managers see, with detailed tabs below for drill-down.

4. **Add tests** for `ManagerAiSummaryCard` and update `TeamWeekPage.test.tsx`:
   - Test rendering with sample summary data.
   - Test loading/error/unavailable states.
   - Test that insights are expanded by default.

steps.title: Extend backend CommitDraftAssist to return suggestedChessPiece
steps.description:
The existing `CommitDraftAssistResponse` returns `suggestedTitle`, `suggestedDescription`, `suggestedSuccessCriteria`, and `suggestedEstimatePoints` but NOT a suggested chess piece. The freeform 'describe → structure' flow (step 4b) needs the AI to propose a chess piece classification from the freeform text. This step adds that capability to the existing endpoint — no new endpoint needed.

1. **Update `CommitDraftAssistResponse.java`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/dto/CommitDraftAssistResponse.java`):
   - Add field `ChessPiece suggestedChessPiece` (nullable) to the record.
   - Update the `unavailable()` factory to include `null` for the new field.

2. **Update the prompt template** (`backend/src/main/resources/prompts/commit-draft-assist.txt`):
   - Add `suggestedChessPiece` to the output JSON schema.
   - Add guidance: 'If chessPiece is null or absent in the input, infer the appropriate chess piece from the title and description. Return null if the current chess piece is already appropriate.'
   - Update all three examples to include the `suggestedChessPiece` field (null when chess piece is already set and appropriate).

3. **Update `CommitDraftAssistService.parseResponse()`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/service/CommitDraftAssistService.java`):
   - Parse the `suggestedChessPiece` string from the AI response JSON.
   - Convert to `ChessPiece` enum via `ChessPiece.valueOf()`, defaulting to `null` on parse failure.
   - Pass it into the `CommitDraftAssistResponse` constructor.

4. **Update frontend type** (`frontend/src/api/aiApi.ts`):
   - Add `suggestedChessPiece?: string | null` to `CommitDraftAssistResponse` interface.

5. **Update existing `CommitDraftAssistButton`** (`frontend/src/components/ai/CommitDraftAssistButton.tsx`):
   - If the response includes `suggestedChessPiece`, add it to the suggestions list with an accept callback (add `onAcceptChessPiece` prop).

6. **Update backend test** (`backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/service/CommitDraftAssistServiceTest.java`):
   - Verify that when the AI response JSON includes `suggestedChessPiece`, it's correctly parsed into the response.
   - Verify that when `suggestedChessPiece` is absent/null, the field is null.

7. **Update frontend test** for `CommitDraftAssistButton` if needed.

steps.title: Add freeform 'describe → structure' AI commit creation mode (frontend)
steps.description:
Today commit creation is a manual form (title, chess piece, RCDO, estimate, success criteria) with an optional AI Suggest button. This step adds a freeform text-first entry point where AI structures the commit. Depends on step 4a for `suggestedChessPiece`.

1. **Create `AiCommitComposer` component** in `frontend/src/components/ai/AiCommitComposer.tsx`:
   - A modal/panel with a large textarea: 'Describe what you're working on this week...'
   - A 'Generate Commit' button that sends the freeform text to `commitDraftAssist` with the freeform text as `currentTitle` and all other fields empty/null (no `chessPiece` input — letting the AI infer it via step 4a).
   - On response, displays the AI-proposed structured commit: suggested title, suggested chess piece (from `suggestedChessPiece`), RCDO match (from parallel `rcdoSuggest` call), estimate points, and success criteria — each as an editable field with `AiSuggestedBadge` (from step 2).
   - User can accept the whole thing (creates the commit) or switch to the manual form with AI values pre-filled.
   - Has a 'Switch to manual form' link for users who prefer the traditional approach.

2. **Add `commitFromFreeform` convenience method** to AI API (`frontend/src/api/aiApi.ts`):
   - Calls `POST /api/ai/commit-draft-assist` with `currentTitle` set to the freeform text, `chessPiece` set to `undefined`, and all other current fields empty.
   - This signals the backend to infer everything including chess piece.

3. **Add RCDO suggestion integration** to `AiCommitComposer`:
   - Also call `POST /api/ai/rcdo-suggest` with the freeform text as `title` to get an RCDO recommendation.
   - Add `rcdoSuggest` method to `createAiApi` in `frontend/src/api/aiApi.ts` (the backend endpoint `POST /api/ai/rcdo-suggest` already exists).
   - Display the suggested RCDO with rationale and confidence.

4. **Add `RcdoSuggestRequest` and `RcdoSuggestResponse` types** to `frontend/src/api/aiApi.ts`:
   - Request: `{ planId: string, userId: string, title: string, description?: string, chessPiece?: string }`
   - Response: `{ aiAvailable: boolean, suggestionAvailable: boolean, suggestedRcdoNodeId?: string, rcdoTitle?: string, confidence?: number, rationale?: string, suggestionId?: string }`

5. **Update MyWeek page** (`frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx`):
   - Change the '+ Add Commit' button behavior: clicking it now opens `AiCommitComposer` by default (when `aiAssistanceEnabled`).
   - Add a secondary action '+ Add manually' that opens the classic CommitForm directly.
   - When AI is unavailable, fall back to CommitForm.

6. **Add `AiCommitComposer` tests** (`frontend/src/__tests__/AiCommitComposer.test.tsx`):
   - Test freeform text entry and AI response display including chess piece.
   - Test accept-all creating a commit with all AI-suggested values.
   - Test switch to manual form with pre-filled values.
   - Test AI unavailable fallback.

steps.title: Surface proactive risk signal banners on My Week page
steps.description:
Today risk signals only show on the Team Week page when a manager selects a member's plan. This step pushes critical risk signals to the IC on their own My Week page as proactive banners.

1. **Create `ProactiveRiskBanner` component** in `frontend/src/components/ai/ProactiveRiskBanner.tsx`:
   - Takes `planId` as prop.
   - Uses the existing `useRiskSignals(planId)` hook from `frontend/src/api/aiHooks.ts`.
   - Filters signals to only show HIGH severity or specific critical types: `OVERCOMMIT`, `BLOCKED_CRITICAL`, `REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD`.
   - Renders as prominent alert banners (not a collapsible panel) with:
     - Risk type icon (reuse from RiskSignalsPanel).
     - Rationale text.
     - Actionable suggestion (e.g., 'Consider reducing scope' or 'Unblock ticket X').
   - Only renders when the plan is LOCKED (risk signals run at lock and daily while locked).
   - Shows nothing when AI is unavailable or no critical signals exist.
   - Includes AiFeedbackButtons for each signal.

2. **Update My Week page** (`frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx`):
   - Add `<ProactiveRiskBanner planId={plan.id} />` between the plan header card and the InsightPanel, only when plan is LOCKED and `aiAssistanceEnabled`.
   - This means critical risks are immediately visible without the user having to navigate elsewhere.

3. **Add tests** (`frontend/src/__tests__/ProactiveRiskBanner.test.tsx`):
   - Test rendering critical signals as banners.
   - Test filtering out LOW severity signals.
   - Test empty state (no critical signals = nothing rendered).
   - Test AI unavailable state.

4. **Update MyWeekPage.test.tsx**:
   - Verify ProactiveRiskBanner renders for locked plans.
   - Verify it doesn't render for draft plans.

steps.title: Final integration verification — build check
steps.description:
Run the full build to verify all 5 AI-core shifts integrate correctly:
- All new components are properly exported and imported.
- No circular dependencies.
- All TypeScript types are consistent across the new AI API additions.
- Frontend builds successfully with Vite.
- Backend builds successfully with Gradle.
- All existing tests still pass with the modified components.

activeSummary.stepTitle: Extend backend CommitDraftAssist to return suggestedChessPiece
